### REQUEST ###
```
POST https://api2.cursor.sh:443/aiserver.v1.AiService/NameTab HTTP/1.1
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Authorization: Bearer REDACTED
Connect-Protocol-Version: 1
Connection: close
Content-Encoding: gzip
Content-Type: application/proto
Cookie: REDACTED
Traceparent: REDACTED
User-Agent: connect-es/1.6.1
X-Amzn-Trace-Id: REDACTED
X-Client-Key: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Checksum: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Client-Arch: x64
X-Cursor-Client-Device-Type: desktop
X-Cursor-Client-Os: win32
X-Cursor-Client-Type: ide
X-Cursor-Client-Version: 2.6.11
X-Cursor-Config-Version: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Streaming: true
X-Cursor-Timezone: REDACTED
X-Ghost-Mode: true
X-New-Onboarding-Completed: false
X-Request-Id: REDACTED
X-Session-Id: REDACTED

{
  "1:LEN": {
    "13:LEN": "REDACTED",
    "1:LEN": "You are implementing a local, production-style job queue + scheduler system (“mini Celery”) intended to stress-test an AI coding agent’s ability to design reliable concurrent systems.\n\nDeliver a complete, runnable repository in Python 3.12 with the following properties:\n- Clear modular architecture (API, broker abstraction, persistence, worker, scheduler, observability, CLI, tests).\n- Deterministic behavior and strong error handling.\n- Extensive automated tests (unit + integration).\n- One-command local startup via Docker Compose.\n\nTechnology constraints (use exactly these unless impossible, then document the exception in README):\n- Language: Python 3.12\n- API: FastAPI + Uvicorn\n- Broker: Redis (use Redis Streams + consumer groups for reliable delivery)\n- Persistence: SQLite (SQLModel or SQLAlchemy); store job metadata and state transitions durably\n- Tests: pytest\n- Formatting/linting: ruff (and mypy if feasible)\n- Observability: structured JSON logs + Prometheus metrics endpoint\n\nSystem overview\nBuild three runnable services plus a CLI:\n1) API service: accepts job submissions and exposes job status, results, and admin actions.\n2) Worker service: consumes jobs from Redis Streams, executes them, records transitions/results, and acknowledges completion.\n3) Scheduler service: enqueues delayed jobs and cron-like recurring jobs.\n4) CLI: submit jobs, inspect status, tail events, and run local load tests.\n\nCore concepts and data model\nJob states: QUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, DEAD\nRequired tables (minimum):\n- jobs: id (UUID), type, payload(JSON), state, created_at, updated_at, idempotency_key (nullable), attempts, max_attempts, next_run_at, schedule (nullable), last_error (nullable), result (nullable JSON)\n- job_events: append-only event log (job_id, ts, old_state, new_state, message, metadata JSON)\n\nBroker semantics (Redis Streams)\n- Use a dedicated stream per queue (e.g., stream “jobs:default”) and a consumer group (e.g., “workers”).\n- Reliable processing requirements:\n  - A job must not be lost if a worker crashes mid-execution.\n  - Implement a visibility-timeout style reclaim: periodically scan PEL (pending entries list) and XCLAIM jobs stuck beyond a configurable timeout.\n  - A job must be acknowledged (XACK) only after the final state transition is durably persisted.\n  - Ensure at-least-once delivery; implement idempotency controls to prevent duplicate side-effects for provided idempotency keys.\n\nAPI requirements\nExpose these endpoints (exact behavior is required):\n- POST /jobs\n  - Request: { \"type\": string, \"payload\": object, \"queue\": \"default\" (optional), \"max_attempts\": int (optional, default 3), \"run_at\": RFC3339 timestamp (optional), \"idempotency_key\": string (optional) }\n  - Behavior:\n    - If idempotency_key already exists for a job in non-terminal state, return that job (do not enqueue a new one).\n    - If run_at is absent, enqueue immediately; otherwise store as delayed and let scheduler enqueue at run_at.\n  - Response: { \"job_id\": uuid }\n- GET /jobs/{job_id}\n  - Returns full job record including state, attempts, result (if terminal), last_error (if any)\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel\n  - If job is QUEUED or delayed (not yet enqueued), mark CANCELED and ensure it will not run\n  - If job is RUNNING, mark as “cancel requested”; worker must check a cancellation flag between steps for cooperative cancellation for supported tasks\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry\n  - Only for FAILED or DEAD jobs; resets state to QUEUED and enqueues again with attempts=0 (or attempts preserved but allowed; choose one and document)\n- GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=\n  - Basic pagination over jobs\n- GET /metrics\n  - Prometheus metrics\n- GET /healthz\n  - Liveness and broker/db connectivity checks\n\nWorker requirements\n- Configurable concurrency: N processes or asyncio tasks (choose one; document)\n- Job execution framework:\n  - Jobs are registered “task types” (e.g., “sleep”, “http_get”, “cpu_burn”, “fail_then_succeed”, “pipeline_demo”)\n  - Each task must validate payload schema (pydantic models)\n  - Record state transitions: QUEUED→RUNNING→(SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD)\n- Retry policy:\n  - Exponential backoff with jitter: base_delay (e.g., 1s), multiplier, cap; configurable per job type\n  - Increment attempts on each execution attempt\n  - On exceeding max_attempts, mark DEAD and push an entry to a dead-letter stream (e.g., “jobs:dlq”)\n- Idempotency:\n  - If idempotency_key is provided, ensure that re-delivered jobs do not re-run side effects if they already SUCCEEDED. Return the prior result instead.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n  - For at least one task type, implement multi-step execution that checks a cancellation flag between steps.\n\nScheduler requirements\nSupport both:\n1) Delayed jobs (run_at): store jobs with next_run_at, and enqueue them when due.\n2) Cron-like recurring jobs:\n   - API to create schedules is optional, but scheduler must support loading schedules from a config file (YAML/JSON) at startup.\n   - Example schedule: every minute enqueue “pipeline_demo” with payload.\nImplementation requirements:\n- Scheduler must be robust to restarts (no duplicate enqueues beyond at-least-once semantics; use a dedupe key per scheduled tick).\n- Store schedule ticks in DB or Redis with a deterministic key (schedule_id + planned_time) to prevent repeated enqueue on restart.\n\nObservability requirements\n- Structured JSON logs for all services with correlation fields:\n  - job_id, stream_id, consumer, attempt, state\n- Prometheus metrics (minimum):\n  - jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}\n  - jobs_started_total{type}\n  - jobs_finished_total{type,state}\n  - job_duration_seconds histogram{type}\n  - queue_depth gauge{queue}\n  - worker_reclaims_total\n  - scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}\n- Include a simple “events tail” in CLI that streams job_events via polling.\n\nRepository requirements\nProvide:\n- docker-compose.yml that starts Redis + API + scheduler + at least 1 worker\n- Makefile (or just scripts) with:\n  - make test\n  - make lint\n  - make up\n  - make down\n  - make loadtest (submit many jobs concurrently and report throughput/latency)\n- README.md with:\n  - Architecture explanation\n  - Failure scenarios and how your design handles them (worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart)\n  - Quickstart commands\n  - How to add a new task type\n  - Clear list of design tradeoffs\n\nTesting requirements (must be automated)\n- Unit tests:\n  - State transition rules\n  - Retry backoff calculation (including jitter bounds)\n  - Idempotency behavior\n- Integration tests (dockerized Redis OK):\n  - At-least-once delivery: simulate worker crash mid-job and verify job is reclaimed and completes\n  - DLQ behavior after max_attempts\n  - Delayed job runs not earlier than run_at\n  - Scheduler restart does not enqueue duplicates for the same scheduled tick\n- All tests must pass via “make test”.\n\nDo not ask me questions. If any detail is ambiguous, choose a reasonable default and document it in README under “Assumptions”.\nDeliver working code, not pseudocode.",
    "2:VARINT": 1,
    "47:VARINT": 2,

### SIGNATURE ###
```
http:host is localhost
http:method is POST
http:queryparams is -NONE-
http:requestBodyJSON is {"1:LEN": {"13:LEN": "REDACTED", "1:LEN": "You are implementing a local, production-style job queue + scheduler system (“mini Celery”) intended to stress-test an AI coding agent’s ability to design reliable concurrent systems.\n\nDeliver a complete, runnable repository in Python 3.12 with the following properties:\n- Clear modular architecture (API, broker abstraction, persistence, worker, scheduler, observability, CLI, tests).\n- Deterministic behavior and strong error handling.\n- Extensive automated tests (unit + integration).\n- One-command local startup via Docker Compose.\n\nTechnology constraints (use exactly these unless impossible, then document the exception in README):\n- Language: Python 3.12\n- API: FastAPI + Uvicorn\n- Broker: Redis (use Redis Streams + consumer groups for reliable delivery)\n- Persistence: SQLite (SQLModel or SQLAlchemy); store job metadata and state transitions durably\n- Tests: pytest\n- Formatting/linting: ruff (and mypy if feasible)\n- Observability: structured JSON logs + Prometheus metrics endpoint\n\nSystem overview\nBuild three runnable services plus a CLI:\n1) API service: accepts job submissions and exposes job status, results, and admin actions.\n2) Worker service: consumes jobs from Redis Streams, executes them, records transitions/results, and acknowledges completion.\n3) Scheduler service: enqueues delayed jobs and cron-like recurring jobs.\n4) CLI: submit jobs, inspect status, tail events, and run local load tests.\n\nCore concepts and data model\nJob states: QUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, DEAD\nRequired tables (minimum):\n- jobs: id (UUID), type, payload(JSON), state, created_at, updated_at, idempotency_key (nullable), attempts, max_attempts, next_run_at, schedule (nullable), last_error (nullable), result (nullable JSON)\n- job_events: append-only event log (job_id, ts, old_state, new_state, message, metadata JSON)\n\nBroker semantics (Redis Streams)\n- Use a dedicated stream per queue (e.g., stream “jobs:default”) and a consumer group (e.g., “workers”).\n- Reliable processing requirements:\n  - A job must not be lost if a worker crashes mid-execution.\n  - Implement a visibility-timeout style reclaim: periodically scan PEL (pending entries list) and XCLAIM jobs stuck beyond a configurable timeout.\n  - A job must be acknowledged (XACK) only after the final state transition is durably persisted.\n  - Ensure at-least-once delivery; implement idempotency controls to prevent duplicate side-effects for provided idempotency keys.\n\nAPI requirements\nExpose these endpoints (exact behavior is required):\n- POST /jobs\n  - Request: { \"type\": string, \"payload\": object, \"queue\": \"default\" (optional), \"max_attempts\": int (optional, default 3), \"run_at\": RFC3339 timestamp (optional), \"idempotency_key\": string (optional) }\n  - Behavior:\n    - If idempotency_key already exists for a job in non-terminal state, return that job (do not enqueue a new one).\n    - If run_at is absent, enqueue immediately; otherwise store as delayed and let scheduler enqueue at run_at.\n  - Response: { \"job_id\": uuid }\n- GET /jobs/{job_id}\n  - Returns full job record including state, attempts, result (if terminal), last_error (if any)\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel\n  - If job is QUEUED or delayed (not yet enqueued), mark CANCELED and ensure it will not run\n  - If job is RUNNING, mark as “cancel requested”; worker must check a cancellation flag between steps for cooperative cancellation for supported tasks\n- POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry\n  - Only for FAILED or DEAD jobs; resets state to QUEUED and enqueues again with attempts=0 (or attempts preserved but allowed; choose one and document)\n- GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=\n  - Basic pagination over jobs\n- GET /metrics\n  - Prometheus metrics\n- GET /healthz\n  - Liveness and broker/db connectivity checks\n\nWorker requirements\n- Configurable concurrency: N processes or asyncio tasks (choose one; document)\n- Job execution framework:\n  - Jobs are registered “task types” (e.g., “sleep”, “http_get”, “cpu_burn”, “fail_then_succeed”, “pipeline_demo”)\n  - Each task must validate payload schema (pydantic models)\n  - Record state transitions: QUEUED→RUNNING→(SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD)\n- Retry policy:\n  - Exponential backoff with jitter: base_delay (e.g., 1s), multiplier, cap; configurable per job type\n  - Increment attempts on each execution attempt\n  - On exceeding max_attempts, mark DEAD and push an entry to a dead-letter stream (e.g., “jobs:dlq”)\n- Idempotency:\n  - If idempotency_key is provided, ensure that re-delivered jobs do not re-run side effects if they already SUCCEEDED. Return the prior result instead.\n- Cooperative cancellation:\n  - For at least one task type, implement multi-step execution that checks a cancellation flag between steps.\n\nScheduler requirements\nSupport both:\n1) Delayed jobs (run_at): store jobs with next_run_at, and enqueue them when due.\n2) Cron-like recurring jobs:\n   - API to create schedules is optional, but scheduler must support loading schedules from a config file (YAML/JSON) at startup.\n   - Example schedule: every minute enqueue “pipeline_demo” with payload.\nImplementation requirements:\n- Scheduler must be robust to restarts (no duplicate enqueues beyond at-least-once semantics; use a dedupe key per scheduled tick).\n- Store schedule ticks in DB or Redis with a deterministic key (schedule_id + planned_time) to prevent repeated enqueue on restart.\n\nObservability requirements\n- Structured JSON logs for all services with correlation fields:\n  - job_id, stream_id, consumer, attempt, state\n- Prometheus metrics (minimum):\n  - jobs_enqueued_total{type,queue}\n  - jobs_started_total{type}\n  - jobs_finished_total{type,state}\n  - job_duration_seconds histogram{type}\n  - queue_depth gauge{queue}\n  - worker_reclaims_total\n  - scheduler_enqueues_total{schedule_id}\n- Include a simple “events tail” in CLI that streams job_events via polling.\n\nRepository requirements\nProvide:\n- docker-compose.yml that starts Redis + API + scheduler + at least 1 worker\n- Makefile (or just scripts) with:\n  - make test\n  - make lint\n  - make up\n  - make down\n  - make loadtest (submit many jobs concurrently and report throughput/latency)\n- README.md with:\n  - Architecture explanation\n  - Failure scenarios and how your design handles them (worker crash, Redis restart, scheduler restart)\n  - Quickstart commands\n  - How to add a new task type\n  - Clear list of design tradeoffs\n\nTesting requirements (must be automated)\n- Unit tests:\n  - State transition rules\n  - Retry backoff calculation (including jitter bounds)\n  - Idempotency behavior\n- Integration tests (dockerized Redis OK):\n  - At-least-once delivery: simulate worker crash mid-job and verify job is reclaimed and completes\n  - DLQ behavior after max_attempts\n  - Delayed job runs not earlier than run_at\n  - Scheduler restart does not enqueue duplicates for the same scheduled tick\n- All tests must pass via “make test”.\n\nDo not ask me questions. If any detail is ambiguous, choose a reasonable default and document it in README under “Assumptions”.\nDeliver working code, not pseudocode.", "2:VARINT": 1, "47:VARINT": 2, "57:LEN": "{\"root\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Extensive automated tests (unit + integration).\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- One-command local startup via Docker Compose.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Technology constraints (use exactly these unless impossible, then document the exception in README):\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Language: Python 3.12\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- API: FastAPI + Uvicorn\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Broker: Redis (use Redis Streams + consumer groups for reliable delivery)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Persistence: SQLite (SQLModel or SQLAlchemy); store job metadata and state transitions durably\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Tests: pytest\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Formatting/linting: ruff (and mypy if feasible)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Observability: structured JSON logs + Prometheus metrics endpoint\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"System overview\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Build three runnable services plus a CLI:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"1) API service: accepts job submissions and exposes job status, results, and admin actions.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"2) Worker service: consumes jobs from Redis Streams, executes them, records transitions/results, and acknowledges completion.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"3) Scheduler service: enqueues delayed jobs and cron-like recurring jobs.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"4) CLI: submit jobs, inspect status, tail events, and run local load tests.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Core concepts and data model\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Job states: QUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, DEAD\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Required tables (minimum):\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- jobs: id (UUID), type, payload(JSON), state, created_at, updated_at, idempotency_key (nullable), attempts, max_attempts, next_run_at, schedule (nullable), last_error (nullable), result (nullable JSON)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- job_events: append-only event log (job_id, ts, old_state, new_state, message, metadata JSON)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Broker semantics (Redis Streams)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Use a dedicated stream per queue (e.g., stream “jobs:default”) and a consumer group (e.g., “workers”).\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Reliable processing requirements:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - A job must not be lost if a worker crashes mid-execution.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Implement a visibility-timeout style reclaim: periodically scan PEL (pending entries list) and XCLAIM jobs stuck beyond a configurable timeout.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - A job must be acknowledged (XACK) only after the final state transition is durably persisted.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Ensure at-least-once delivery; implement idempotency controls to prevent duplicate side-effects for provided idempotency keys.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"API requirements\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Expose these endpoints (exact behavior is required):\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- POST /jobs\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Request: { \\\"type\\\": string, \\\"payload\\\": object, \\\"queue\\\": \\\"default\\\" (optional), \\\"max_attempts\\\": int (optional, default 3), \\\"run_at\\\": RFC3339 timestamp (optional), \\\"idempotency_key\\\": string (optional) }\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Behavior:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"    - If idempotency_key already exists for a job in non-terminal state, return that job (do not enqueue a new one).\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"    - If run_at is absent, enqueue immediately; otherwise store as delayed and let scheduler enqueue at run_at.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Response: { \\\"job_id\\\": uuid }\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- GET /jobs/{job_id}\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Returns full job record including state, attempts, result (if terminal), last_error (if any)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- POST /jobs/{job_id}/cancel\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - If job is QUEUED or delayed (not yet enqueued), mark CANCELED and ensure it will not run\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - If job is RUNNING, mark as “cancel requested”; worker must check a cancellation flag between steps for cooperative cancellation for supported tasks\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- POST /jobs/{job_id}/retry\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Only for FAILED or DEAD jobs; resets state to QUEUED and enqueues again with attempts=0 (or attempts preserved but allowed; choose one and document)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- GET /jobs?state=&type=&limit=&cursor=\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Basic pagination over jobs\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- GET /metrics\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Liveness and broker/db connectivity checks\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Worker requirements\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Configurable concurrency: N processes or asyncio tasks (choose one; document)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Job execution framework:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Jobs are registered “task types” (e.g., “sleep”, “http_get”, “cpu_burn”, “fail_then_succeed”, “pipeline_demo”)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Each task must validate payload schema (pydantic models)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Record state transitions: QUEUED→RUNNING→(SUCCEEDED|FAILED|CANCELED|DEAD)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Retry policy:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Exponential backoff with jitter: base_delay (e.g., 1s), multiplier, cap; configurable per job type\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - Increment attempts on each execution attempt\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - On exceeding max_attempts, mark DEAD and push an entry to a dead-letter stream (e.g., “jobs:dlq”)\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Idempotency:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - If idempotency_key is provided, ensure that re-delivered jobs do not re-run side effects if they already SUCCEEDED. Return the prior result instead.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Cooperative cancellation:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"  - For at least one task type, implement multi-step execution that checks a cancellation flag between steps.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [], \"direction\": null, \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Support both:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"1) Delayed jobs (run_at): store jobs with next_run_at, and enqueue them when due.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"2) Cron-like recurring jobs:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"   - API to create schedules is optional, but scheduler must support loading schedules from a config file (YAML/JSON) at startup.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"   - Example schedule: every minute enqueue “pipeline_demo” with payload.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"Implementation requirements:\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Scheduler must be robust to restarts (no duplicate enqueues beyond at-least-once semantics; use a dedupe key per scheduled tick).\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [{\"detail\": 0, \"format\": 0, \"mode\": \"normal\", \"style\": \"\", \"text\": \"- Store schedule ticks in DB or Redis with a deterministic key (schedule_id + planned_time) to prevent repeated enqueue on restart.\", \"type\": \"text\", \"version\": 1}], \"direction\": \"ltr\", \"format\": \"\", \"indent\": 0, \"type\": \"paragraph\", \"version\": 1}, {\"children\": [],
